title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
While visiting the home of Louisa Wallace, Hercule Poirot examines a portrait of Louisa painted by Nancy Duane. Louisa explains that both Nancy and her husband, Saint John Wallace, are talented artists with a competitive streak; while Saint John’s delicate paintings decorate the house, Poirot observes that Nancy’s work is more expressive. During the visit, the maid, Dulcinites (also referred to as Dorcas), accidentally spills coffee on her apron while serving Poirot.

Poirot becomes preoccupied with a specific detail in Nancy’s portrait: a set of blue pots and bowls. He requests a tour of the house to view Saint John’s many works. While viewing a painting titled "Blue Field Bindweed: Euphorbia," Poirot realizes a connection that causes his heart to race. He leads Louisa to the room where Nancy painted the portrait, where he notices a set of white pots and bowls with red, green, and white decorations on a wooden table. 

Poirot questions Louisa about the blue pots and bowls depicted in the portrait. Louisa realizes the blue items are missing and accuses Dulcinites of theft, suggesting the maid must have moved or broken them, but Poirot defends the maid's innocence. Poirot then questions if Nancy Duane took the items when she left on Thursday evening, but Louisa dismisses this, noting that Nancy left with nothing but a key. Despite the confusion regarding the missing items, Poirot asks to return to the portrait to continue his investigation.
